1.2. Aims and Objective of the Work
1.2.1. Aims of "Computer Vision Lip Reading System"
The Computer Vision Lip Reading System is designed to recognize spoken words from visual lip movemen...
Visual Speech Recognition: To develop a system that can accurately recognize spoken words solely fro...
Automated Lip Detection & Frame Capture: To build an intelligent pipeline that automatically detects...
Deep Learning Classification: To train a 3D Convolutional Neural Network capable of learning spatiot...
Real-Time Inference: To enable real-time word prediction from a live webcam feed, providing immediat...
In short, the project aims to demonstrate that computer vision and deep learning can be combined to ...
1.2.2. Objectives of "Computer Vision Lip Reading System"
The specific technical objectives of this project are:
Implement Facial Landmark Detection: Utilize a pre-trained shape predictor with 68 facial landmarks ...
Build a Custom Dataset: Design and implement a data collection pipeline that uses lip-distance thres...
Develop Image Preprocessing Pipeline: Implement a robust preprocessing chain including LAB color spa...
Design and Train a 3D CNN Model: Architect and train a sequential deep learning model with three 3D ...
Implement Live Prediction System: Build a real-time prediction module that captures webcam frames, d...
Implement File-Based Prediction: Build a video file prediction module that processes pre-recorded vi...

The development of this Lip Reading System is grounded in existing research and innovations across t...

Visual speech recognition (VSR), also known as automatic lip reading, has been an active area of res...

The breakthrough application of deep learning to lip reading came with the LipNet model (Assael et a...

The facial landmark detector used in this project, based on the work of Kazemi and Sullivan (2014) "...
1.4. Problem Definition
The current landscape of speech recognition and accessibility technology faces several critical chal...
Audio Dependency of Speech Recognition: Mainstream speech recognition systems (Google Speech-to-Text...
Limited Accessibility for Hearing-Impaired: While sign language recognition has received research at...
Dataset Scarcity for Word-Level Lip Reading: Most existing lip reading datasets (GRID, LRW, LRS) are...
Real-Time Processing Challenge: Performing facial landmark detection, lip region extraction, image p...
1.5. Plan of the Work
To ensure the successful development of the Computer Vision Lip Reading System, the project work is ...
Phase 1: Requirement Analysis & Feasibility
Reviewing literature on visual speech recognition, 3D CNNs, and facial landmark detection.
Defining the vocabulary: 13 common English words.
Determining the technical stack: Python, TensorFlow/Keras, OpenCV, dlib, NumPy.
Evaluating feasibility of real-time lip reading on consumer hardware.
Phase 2: Data Collection & Dataset Creation
Designing the data collection pipeline with automatic speech detection via lip-distance thresholds.
Implementing the data collection module with calibration mode, circular frame buffers, and standardi...
Collecting ~700 video clips across 13 word classes using webcam recordings.
Organizing data into labeled folders with structured array files and video files.
Phase 3: Model Design & Training
Designing the 3D CNN architecture with 3D convolutional, max-pooling, fully connected, and dropout l...
Implementing data loading, label encoding, and train/test split (80/20).
Training the model for 20 epochs with Adam optimizer and categorical cross-entropy loss.
Evaluating with confusion matrix, classification report, ROC-AUC curves, and balanced accuracy.
Phase 4: Inference System Development
Building the real-time prediction module for webcam-based word prediction.
Building the file-based prediction module for batch inference on pre-recorded video files.
Implementing the same preprocessing pipeline used during training for inference consistency.
Phase 5: Testing & Validation
Testing with various speakers, lighting conditions, and camera angles.
Validating model accuracy against the test set (98.5% validation accuracy achieved).
Recording demonstration videos to showcase system capabilities.

2.3. Problem Statement & Gaps
Accessibility Gap: Despite 430 million people with hearing loss, no mainstream consumer application ...
Complexity Gap: Existing deep learning lip reading systems (LipNet, DeepMind) require massive datase...
Dataset Gap: Most lip reading datasets (GRID, LRW, LRS) feature controlled studio conditions and are...
Audio-Free Gap: The vast majority of speech recognition systems require audio input. A purely visual...
2.4. The Role of Deep Learning Intervention (The Project's Contribution)

End-to-End Pipeline: The project delivers a complete pipeline from data collection → preprocessing →...
Robust Preprocessing: The LAB-CLAHE-Gaussian-Bilateral-Sharpening preprocessing pipeline addresses t...
3    SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS STUDY
3.1  USER CHARACTERISTICS

3.3  ASSUMPTIONS AND DEPENDENCIES
3.1. User Characteristics
The Computer Vision Lip Reading System is designed for two distinct user groups, each with specific ...
3.1.1. Hearing-Impaired Individuals & Communication Aid Users (Primary Users)
Role: End users who benefit from real-time visual lip reading for communication assistance.
Characteristics:
Demographics: Individuals with partial or complete hearing loss of all ages.
Technical Expertise: Low to Medium. The system is designed for simple usage — run a script and speak...
Frequency of Use: Regular (daily conversations, meetings, educational settings).
Key Privileges:
Use live prediction mode to see real-time word recognition from webcam.
View predicted words displayed on screen.
Reset prediction history for new conversations.
3.1.2. Researchers & Developers (Secondary Users)
Role: Users who extend, modify, or build upon the system for research or development.
Characteristics:
Demographics: Computer science students, machine learning researchers, accessibility technology deve...
Technical Expertise: High. Familiar with Python, deep learning, and computer vision.
Frequency of Use: Periodic (during research, model retraining, dataset expansion).
Key Privileges:
Use data collection mode to record new training data for additional words.
Retrain the model with expanded datasets using the Jupyter notebook.
Modify preprocessing parameters and model architecture.
Use file-based prediction for batch processing of video files.
3.2. Hardware and Software Requirements
3.2.1. Hardware Requirements
Client-Side (User Machine)
Processor: Intel Core i5 or equivalent (quad-core 2.0 GHz minimum). GPU recommended for training (NV...
RAM: Minimum 8 GB (16 GB recommended for model training).
Webcam: Built-in or external USB webcam with minimum 480p resolution (720p recommended).
Storage: Minimum 5 GB free space (3 GB for dataset, 1 GB for model weights, 1 GB for dependencies).
Display: Standard monitor for viewing the webcam feed and prediction results.
Training Environment (Optional)
The training was performed on Kaggle using cloud GPU (NVIDIA Tesla P100 or T4).
Local training requires an NVIDIA GPU with at least 4 GB VRAM and CUDA toolkit installed.
3.2.2. Software Requirements
Development/Runtime Environment
Operating System: Windows 10/11, macOS (Catalina or later), or Linux (Ubuntu 18.04+).
Python: Version 3.11 or 3.12.
Code Editor: Visual Studio Code, Cursor IDE, or Jupyter Notebook (for training).
3.3. Assumptions and Dependencies
3.3.1. Assumptions
Webcam Availability: It is assumed that the user's machine has a functioning webcam accessible by th...
Frontal Face Orientation: The system assumes the user is facing the camera approximately frontally. ...
Adequate Lighting: It is assumed that the user's face is reasonably well-lit. While the contrast enh...
Single Speaker: The current implementation processes one face at a time (the first detected face). I...
English Words Only: The system is trained exclusively on 13 English words. It assumes all spoken inp...
Consistent Speaking Style: The system assumes a natural speaking pace and mouth movement. Exaggerate...
3.3.2. Dependencies
Pre-trained Facial Landmark Model: The system depends on a pre-trained 68-point facial landmark mode...
Trained Neural Network Weights: The trained 3D CNN weight files must be present in the model directo...
Webcam Interface: The webcam capture depends on the computer vision library interfacing with the sys...
Package Compatibility: The project depends on specific versions of the deep learning framework, faci...
GPU Drivers (Training Only): If retraining the model locally, the system depends on NVIDIA CUDA driv...

5.3. Data Integrity and Processing
Frame Count Consistency: Every word sample is guaranteed to contain exactly 22 frames. The data coll...
Spatial Consistency: All lip frames are resized to exactly 112×80 pixels using standard image resizi...
Preprocessing Pipeline: Every frame undergoes the same preprocessing chain:
LAB Color Space Conversion for luminance-chrominance separation
CLAHE Contrast Enhancement (clip limit of 3.0, tile grid of 3×3) on the luminance channel
Conversion back to the original color space
Gaussian Blur (7×7 kernel) for initial noise reduction
Bilateral Filtering for edge-preserving smoothing
Sharpening via a custom convolution kernel
Final Gaussian Blur (5×5 kernel) for subtle smoothing
Data Types: Raw frames are stored as lists of integers (0-255) in serialized format. For model input...
Train/Test Split: The dataset is split 80% training / 20% validation using a stratified random split...

4. Frame Assembly:
Each captured word is assembled into a fixed 22-frame sequence. This includes 4 pre-speech buffer fr...
5. Preprocessing and Storage:
Each of the 22 lip-region frames undergoes the standardized preprocessing pipeline (color space conv...
6. Quality Control:

6.2. Conclusion
This project successfully demonstrates that visual-only lip reading using 3D Convolutional Neural Ne...
The project validates several key technical achievements:
A custom-built data collection pipeline can efficiently generate high-quality lip reading training d...
3D CNNs are effective at capturing spatiotemporal features from lip movement sequences, enabling acc...
The multi-stage preprocessing pipeline (color space conversion, contrast enhancement, smoothing, bil...
The complete pipeline from data collection → training → real-time inference can run on standard cons...
Future enhancements planned for subsequent iterations include:
Expanding the vocabulary beyond 13 words to support a larger set of common English words.
Implementing sentence-level lip reading by combining the word-level model with a language model for ...
Adding speaker-independent training by collecting data from multiple speakers to improve generalizat...
Implementing a graphical user interface (GUI) for easier usage by non-technical users.
Exploring transfer learning from larger pre-trained video models (e.g., I3D, SlowFast) to improve ac...
Deploying as a web application or mobile app for broader accessibility.
Integrating with assistive technology frameworks for hearing-impaired users.
